title: Add atomic file write utility
description: Implements an atomic file write to prevent data corruption on crash during config updates.
diff:
diff --git a/utils/atomic_write.py b/utils/atomic_write.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d0e1f2a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/utils/atomic_write.py
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+import os
+import tempfile
+
+def atomic_write(path: str, data: str) -> None:
+    # Check if file exists and is writable
+    if os.path.exists(path) and not os.access(path, os.W_OK):
+        raise PermissionError(f"Cannot write to {path}")
+
+    # TOCTOU: Between the exists/access check above and the write below,
+    # the file could be replaced by a symlink to a sensitive file
+    
+    tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=os.path.dirname(path))
+    with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, 'w') as f:
+        f.write(data)
+    # Rename is atomic on POSIX, but the permission check above is racy
+    os.rename(tmp_path, path)
comments: adam: Good atomic write pattern. Approve.

security-beth: TOCTOU vulnerability. Between os.path.exists()/os.access() and the actual write, an attacker could replace the target path with a symlink to /etc/passwd or another sensitive file. The subsequent rename would overwrite the symlink target.
carl: Also os.access() checks are unreliable for security decisions due to race conditions. Remove the pre-check entirely and handle errors on the write itself.
author-dana: I'll remove the pre-check and add O_NOFOLLOW flags.
author: dana
